use_cases: Provides quick access to frequently used actions without cluttering the main interface
Enables compact mobile-first navigation with expandable options on tap
Serves as a space-efficient alternative to traditional toolbars or sidebars
Allows users to trigger contextual actions from anywhere on the screen
Reduces cognitive load by organizing related actions behind a single trigger
ai_summary: A floating action menu component that displays a primary trigger button which expands on click to reveal a list of contextual actions or navigation options. This space-efficient pattern is ideal for mobile-first interfaces and desktop applications needing quick access to secondary actions without consuming fixed UI space. Built with Radix UI primitives for accessibility and class-variance-authority for variant theming.
